axioms.name: Mimicry of Life
axioms.statement: Drama (Natya) is mimicry (anukarana) of the exploits of gods, Asuras, kings, and householders.

axioms.name: Satisfying Diverse Tastes
axioms.statement: Drama exists to provide satisfaction to people of differing tastes in one place.

axioms.name: The Soul of Sentiment
axioms.statement: The Sentiment (rasa) is the soul of drama—no meaning proceeds without it.

axioms.name: States Precede Sentiments
axioms.statement: Sentiments arise from States (bhava), not States from Sentiments.

axioms.name: The Culinary Metaphor
axioms.statement: Aesthetic experience is tasted (asvadayanti)—like culinary flavor from combined ingredients.

axioms.name: The Two Practices
axioms.statement: Successful representation requires both realistic (lokadharmi) and conventional (natyadharmi) practices.

axioms.name: The Court of the People
axioms.statement: The ultimate court of appeal concerning dramatic practice is the people.

core_algorithms.name: Rasa Evocation Formula
core_algorithms.purpose: Produce aesthetic experience through the synthesis of emotional components
core_algorithms.pseudocode: sthayibhava = identify_dominant_state(target_rasa); vibhavas = select_causes(target_rasa); anubhavas = select_expressions(target_rasa); vyabhicaris = filter_compatible_transitory_states(target_rasa); RETURN combine_through_abhinaya(sthayibhava, vibhavas, anubhavas, vyabhicaris)

core_algorithms.name: Practice Selection Algorithm
core_algorithms.purpose: Determine appropriate practice (realistic vs conventional) for a scene
core_algorithms.pseudocode: IF sentiment IN [erotic, heroic, furious, marvellous] OR scene_type IN [combat, love_making]: RETURN natyadharmi (stylized); ELIF scene_type == domestic: RETURN lokadharmi (realistic); ELSE: RETURN blend(realistic, conventional, intensity)

core_algorithms.name: Five Junctures (Sandhi)
core_algorithms.purpose: Sequence the plot through its five essential phases of action
core_algorithms.pseudocode: 1. MUKHA: plant germ (bija); 2. PRATIMUKHA: advance action (bindu); 3. GARBHA: unfold episodes (pataka); 4. VIMARSA: reach crisis/pause (niyatapti); 5. NIRVAHANA: achieve result (phalagama);

core_algorithms.name: Dhruva-Narrative Synchronization
core_algorithms.purpose: Align musical cues with narrative punctuation and transitions
core_algorithms.pseudocode: IF event == entrance: APPLY pravesiki; ELIF event == exit: APPLY niskramiki; ELIF event == resolution: APPLY prasadiki; ELIF event == transition: APPLY antara; ELSE: APPLY aksepiki; MATCH tempo(dhruva) TO rasa(scene)

core_algorithms.name: Theory of Blemishes (Failure Modes)
core_algorithms.purpose: Identify and categorize failure modes in dramatic representation
core_algorithms.pseudocode: FOR each_defect IN performance: IF source == performers: RETURN self_made_blemish(remedy_available); IF source == external: RETURN accidental_blemish(ignore_or_halt); IF severity == moderate: impact = spoil_success; RETURN blemish_report(severity, source, remedy)

diagnostic_questions.question: Which Dominant State (sthayibhava) is being evoked?
Are the Determinants (causes) specific and appropriate?
Are the Consequents (expressions) appropriate to the sentiment?
Are the Transitory States compatible with the dominant rasa?
Does the representation achieve universality (rasa) over personal emotion?
Is the Germ (bija) planted in the Opening?
Do the Five Junctures appear in logical sequence?
Does the Incidental Plot INCIDENTALLY serve the Principal Plot?
Do the four Abhinaya modes (body, word, appearance, mind) work in harmony?
Is the practice (dharmi) appropriate to the emotional intensity?
Are prohibited on-stage actions (death, eating, battle) reported rather than shown?

maps_to: South Park's 'Therefore/But' logic (Causal binding in Sandhi)

core_operations: Select DHARMI based on intensity: high = natya, grounded = loka
Sequence Action: Opening -> Progress -> Development -> Pause -> Conclusion
Represent the invisible through Sattvika (mind-born) involuntary response
key_constraints: Each act must fit within a single day
All four modes of Abhinaya must harmonize
Drama must satisfy diverse tastes in one place
Prohibited actions must be reported, never shown
